title: Fall of Desevro

The '''fall''' of the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Desevro/Legends|Desevro]] occurred around [[23,900 BBY]], at the end of the [[Standard century/Legends|century]]-long [[Tionese War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion|Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion]]. The Republic, radicalized by the fanatically persistent fighting of its [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] enemy, carried out [[pressure bomb]] attacks against the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] worlds. Desevro was the last to fall to the Republic after suffering extensive devastation and offered the unconditional surrender of the Honorable Union.

==Prelude==
[[File:Desevro.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Desevro, the capital of the Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion]]
For almost a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] beginning in [[24,000 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion|Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion]] fought a conflict known as the [[Tionese War]]. The war saw powerful [[pressure bomb]]s being dropped on both [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="EGTW" /> and [[Desevro/Legends|Desevro]], the respective [[Capital/Legends|capital]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the opposing sides. Eventually, the [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] leaders felt that their peoples' culture faced extinction due to the technological superiority of the Republic as well as the threat posed by the<ref name="EGTW" /> [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster's]] neighboring [[Hutt Empire]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

As a result, the Tionese kept fanatically fighting on, subjecting Republic worlds and citizens to sneak attacks, raids, and [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] acts. In response, the Republic also radicalized and adopted a strategy of total war, storming up the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] and devastating several worlds in the Tion through the use of pressure [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==The attack==
Around [[23,900 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> Desevro was also blasted by the Republic forces, in the process suffering extensive devastation. The planet was the last of the Tionese worlds to fall, finally offering the unconditional surrender of the Honorable Union.<ref name="EGTW" />

==Aftermath==
Initially, the military leaders of the Republic had decided to sterilize Desevro as an object lesson to the rest of the Tionese and so refused the surrender. The refusal, however, led the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to break with the Republic. An organization of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] who had pledged to protect the Republic and had entered the Tionese War as Republic [[Commander/Legends|commanders]], the Order could not tolerate being party to the Republic's intended barbaric actions, and tense meetings were held above Desevro between the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the Republic leadership.<ref name="EGTW" />

[[File:FalangMinor-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|280px|In the aftermath of Desevro's fall, the Jedi agreed to watch over the Tion Cluster from a series of fortress worlds along the area's Coreward borders.]]
The Jedi ultimately agreed to [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|monitor]] the Tion Cluster from an arc<ref name="EGTW" /> of Republic [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]]<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> situated along the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Coreward]] borders of the [[Star cluster/Legends|cluster]]. The surrender of the Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion ended the Tionese War and led to the eventual incorporation of the Tion Cluster into Republic territory.<ref name="EGTW" /> Desevro's surrender also denied a young [[Unidentified Republic soldier (Falang Minor)|Republic soldier]], who had enlisted with the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] just four [[Standard month/Legends|months]] earlier, the opportunity to avenge in battle the Tionese [[Attack on Okator VIII|destruction]] of their [[Unidentified farm (Okator VIII)|home]] on [[Okator VIII]].<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-4-the-rise-of-the-republic-needs-author-entry|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 4: The Rise of the Republic}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The fall of Desevro was first mentioned in the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', authored by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]].<ref name="EGTW" />
